title: Scene 1 — Holmes entrance / exit
notes: Holmes faces front, turns to profile, then walks frame-right and exits. Plain single-subject silhouette.

title: Scene 2 — Moriarty menacing (I)
notes: Moriarty stands menacing at the table, then walks frame-left and exits. Plain single-subject silhouette.

title: Scene 3 — Moriarty menacing (II)
notes: Moriarty enters frame-left, walks to centre, holds centre menacing/gesturing, then walks frame-right. Plain single-subject silhouette. Walk-in/out leg-gap timing de-popped so the legs separate on the same frame as the source instead of snapping open a couple frames late.

title: Scene 4 — Séance: Moriarty flickers to the Ghost
notes: Moriarty (man, coat, bowler) flickers out; the Ghost (woman, hooded cloak) flickers in. Flicker-swap effect straddles the transformation.

title: Scene 5 — The Falls: fight + falling deerstalker
notes: Moriarty solo, Holmes enters, the grapple at the falls, the deerstalker falls and lands, Holmes downed, Moriarty exits, Holmes returns alone. Single union silhouette.

title: Scene 6 — Moriarty reaches for Holmes
notes: Moriarty stands center and reaches his right arm toward off-frame Holmes. Plain single-subject silhouette.

note: Filmed before we did it with a scene read. Some are pretty good and we may want to use them. Each is shown twice: the raw footage (for the performance) and its silhouette version. All cut from IMG_2158.MOV. Silhouette versions are pending projectionist review.

note: Reworks of the Moriarty->Ghost transition (p.76). The current approved Scene 4 above reads too 'flashy'. Two batches of alternatives — watch the last ~3 seconds of each, then pick one (or ask for a tweak); the approved clip above is unchanged until a pick is made. BATCH 1 (V1-V3): he deliberately FLICKERS OUT a few times, then the Ghost POPS in. BATCH 2 (V4-V6): the flicker ACCELERATES — fast, then much faster — before the swap.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V1: “Dying bulb”
notes: He blinks fully OUT and back 3 times, the gaps widening each time, then he's gone — a short empty-stage beat — then the Ghost SNAPS in solid. Cleanest read; no ghost peek-through.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V2: “Two worlds”
notes: Same flicker-outs, but in the last gaps the Ghost shows THROUGH instead of white — the two figures phase man↔ghost, one last Moriarty flash 'fighting', then gone and the Ghost pops. Most dramatic; a touch busier.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V3: “Slow collapse”
notes: Only 2 long, slow flicker-outs and a longer fade to gone, then the Ghost MATERIALIZES (one quick blink) and holds. Most deliberate / séance-y.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V4: “Accelerando”
notes: The flicker accelerates smoothly — fast, then much faster — until he snaps out; a short empty beat, then the Ghost hard-pops in solid.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V5: “Two gears”
notes: Two distinct speeds: a few fast flickers, then a much-faster burst, then gone and the Ghost pops. Same idea as V4 but in two clear 'gears' instead of a smooth ramp.

title: Scene 4 — candidate V6: “Charge + materialize”
notes: Moriarty accelerates out (like V4), then the energy passes to the Ghost — she flickers IN fast and settles solid, rather than a single hard pop.

note: Black-on-white title card to bring up on the scene-1 scream (the cold-open hand-off into 1.2). The show title writes on by hand — ink appears in reading order with a soft pen-tip nib — holds, then fades. Waterfall mist drifts up the left & right edges. 8s (write ~4s / hold ~3s / fade ~1s), 1920×1080 60fps, drops straight into the projection set. Three handwriting faces below — watch the motion and pick a face (or ask for a tweak: wording, line breaks, a byline/'Act One' line, mist amount, timing).

title: Title card — A: Snell Roundhand (elegant Victorian script)
notes: Formal copperplate hand — most period / 'handwritten letter' feel. Connected script, so the write-on reads most like a pen moving. Ornate; judge legibility from the back of the house.

title: Title card — B: Bradley Hand (legible casual hand)
notes: Bold casual handwriting — the most legible of the three, more modern feel. Letters are separate, so the write-on reads as ink filling in left-to-right.

title: Title card — C: Noteworthy (clean casual hand)
notes: Clean, even casual hand — legible and friendly, a middle ground between A and B.
